name: Fangzhang
description: Fangzhang (方丈), also known as Penglai (蓬萊) in some accounts, is one of the three legendary Penglai Mountains or isles located in the Bohai Sea, off the eastern coast of China. These islands were believed to be the abodes of the immortals (xian) and were highly sought after by emperors and seekers of immortality throughout Chinese history. Fangzhang, along with Penglai and Yingzhou, were thought to be places of unparalleled beauty and tranquility, where the air was pure, the fruits granted eternal life, and the inhabitants possessed supernatural powers. The islands were often described as being shrouded in mist, making them difficult to find, and those who did manage to reach them were said to be forever changed. The pursuit of Fangzhang and the other Penglai Mountains became a major theme in Daoist alchemy and imperial quests for longevity. Emperor Qin Shi Huang, in particular, famously sent expeditions to search for these isles and the elixir of life they were said to hold. The islands represent a utopian ideal and the human desire to transcend mortality. They are frequently depicted in art and literature as symbols of paradise and the ultimate goal of spiritual attainment. The mythical nature of Fangzhang underscores the importance of seeking balance and harmony with nature in Chinese philosophy and the enduring appeal of immortality.
subtitle: Island of Immortals and Eternal Life

keyMyths.title: Qin Shi Huang's Quest
keyMyths.description: Emperor Qin Shi Huang sent expeditions to find Fangzhang and obtain the elixir of life, highlighting the allure of immortality. These expeditions were ultimately unsuccessful, adding to the island's mystique.

keyMyths.title: The Elusive Island
keyMyths.description: Many stories tell of sailors attempting to find Fangzhang, only to be thwarted by dense fogs or deceptive mirages. This elusiveness underscores the difficulty of achieving true immortality.

sources.author: Sima Qian
sources.description: Provides historical context for Qin Shi Huang's search for the Penglai Islands.
